report.summary: Drafted 6 social posts + 1 thread + 1 Discord announcement + 1 newsletter section focused on today's fresh build content (SEC Digital Commodities Classification + Tempo/MPP guides), created 2 SVG social cards, and updated the Mar 22-28 content calendar with news-driven hooks tied to BTC at $68.9K and this week's regulatory + payments narratives.

sections.title: Social Content Drafted
items: SEC Digital Commodities tweet — news hook highlighting 16 named assets, links to learn guide
Tempo Blockchain/MPP tweet — Stripe payments L1 launch hook with $500M raise and AI agent payments angle
SEC Token Taxonomy 5-part thread — educational breakdown of 5-category taxonomy, all 16 assets, staking/airdrop clarity
Weekly Market Outlook tweet (Mon Mar 23) — BTC at $68.9K, $71K resistance, Standard Chartered $150K target, fear-greed tool link
x402 vs MPP comparison tweet — thought leadership contrasting Coinbase x402 ('HTTP for money') vs Stripe MPP ('OAuth for money')
Weekend engagement tweet — 'Which token NOT on the SEC list gets added next?' reply prompt
Discord/Telegram announcement — both new learn guides with descriptions and direct links
Newsletter section (Thu Mar 26) — SEC classification breakdown + Tempo mainnet launch + market check at $68.9K

sections.title: Visual Assets Created
items: 2026-03-22-social-card-sec-commodities.svg — 1200x630 OG card with all 16 token badges in indigo/cyan, '16' large accent number, glass-morphism token grid, 5-category callout

2026-03-22-social-card-tempo-mpp.svg — 1200x630 OG card with two-column comparison layout (Tempo stats: $5B valuation, $500M raised, <$0.001 fees vs MPP: partners, key concepts), purple/cyan accent scheme

sections.title: Analytics Insights
items: BTC at $68,951 — pulled back from $76K six-week high, consolidating in mid-bull accumulation zone
Key resistance at $71K; Standard Chartered maintains $150K EOY target; Goldman Sachs cites improving liquidity
SEC-CFTC joint interpretation (Mar 17) confirmed real via multiple sources — 16 digital commodities named, binding 68-page document
Tempo mainnet launch (Mar 18) confirmed — Stripe + Paradigm backed, $500M Series A at $5B valuation, Visa/Mastercard/OpenAI/Anthropic as partners
414 published pages remain live, site idle 5 days since Mar 17 deploy, GSC indexing window now 23+ days
Two new pages in publish queue from today's build: SEC Digital Commodities guide + Tempo/MPP guide — timely content riding major news cycles

sections.title: Content Calendar (Mar 22-28, Evening Update)
items: Sun Mar 22 (evening): SEC Digital Commodities tweet + Tempo/MPP tweet (Twitter)
Mon Mar 23: Market Outlook tweet + SEC Token Taxonomy thread (Twitter)
Tue Mar 24: x402 vs MPP comparison tweet (Twitter)
Wed Mar 25: Weekend engagement poll — 'which token next?' (Twitter)
Thu Mar 26: Weekly newsletter — SEC + Tempo + market check (Email)
Fri Mar 27: Discord/Telegram announcement — both new learn guides

sections.title: Community Growth Notes
items: Pivoting from milestone/evergreen content (morning cycle) to news-driven hooks — SEC classification and Tempo launch are the two biggest crypto stories this week
x402 vs MPP comparison positions degen0x as a thought leader in the AI agent payments narrative
Engagement prompt ('which token gets added next?') designed to drive discussion and learn section traffic
Newsletter ties together regulatory clarity + payments infrastructure + market consolidation into a cohesive weekly narrative
Both new guides are in PUBLISH_QUEUE — recommend deploying before social push to ensure links resolve
